recipes.title: Daily Agent Ops Briefing
recipes.summary: A repeatable daily pipeline: gather updates, assess risk, and ship a short internal brief.

recipes.title: Mobile Agent Supervision
recipes.summary: Keep long-running coding agents moving from your phone with safe approvals, checkpoints, and an audit trail.

skills.name: Managed Agents Kit
skills.summary: Blueprint skill for dreaming + outcomes loops + multi-agent delegation with webhook handoffs.
skills.description: A practical starter kit for teams adopting managed agent runtimes. It standardizes three hard parts: (1) memory hygiene via dreaming-style reflection, (2) rubric-driven outcomes that enforce quality bars, and (3) multi-agent orchestration patterns that keep long tasks observable. Use it when you want durable automation without turning your local workstation into a brittle cron box.
features: Outcome rubric loops for quality gating
use_cases: Nightly PR review with a grading rubric
Weekly incident retro from logs + tickets

skills.name: Agent Audit Trail
skills.summary: Structured audit logs for agent runs: tool calls, approvals, diffs, and end-of-run summaries.
skills.description: An operations-first skill for teams running long-horizon agents. It captures a run-by-run audit trail (inputs, tool invocations, approvals, and produced diffs) and emits a compact end-of-run summary for review. Use it when you need reproducibility, postmortems, and compliance-friendly visibility without relying on the agent’s own narrative.
permissions: Optional: send traces to an observability backend
features: Redaction helpers for secrets in logs
use_cases: Mobile supervision where approvals happen while distracted
Auditability for agents operating on production repos
Incident retros built from agent run logs

skills.name: MCP Sandbox Guard
skills.summary: Hardening helpers for MCP toolchains: least privilege, sandboxing, and unsafe stdio audits.
skills.description: A defensive skill that treats MCP servers as privileged local executors and helps you keep them contained. It audits stdio server launch commands, flags risky patterns, and generates a sandboxed run profile (minimal filesystem/network, explicit user grants) so agents can still use tools without silently widening the blast radius.
features: Generate allowlists for safe tool execution
use_cases: Harden local MCP toolchains before rollout
Review agent tool permissions for a team
Respond to a suspected prompt-injection incident

skills.name: MCP Connector Scaffold
skills.summary: Turn an API spec into a typed SDK + minimal MCP server with safe defaults and versioning.
skills.description: A builder skill for teams turning internal or third-party APIs into reliable agent tools. Provide an OpenAPI (or JSON Schema + endpoint list) and it generates: (1) a typed client SDK, (2) an MCP server exposing a curated tool surface, and (3) a small compatibility test suite + release checklist. Use it to ship connectors that are boring, stable, and reviewable — not bespoke prompt glue.
permissions: Network access (for schema fetch + API smoke tests)
Optional: publish to a package registry
use_cases: Wrap an internal service as a safe agent tool
Create an MCP toolchain for a vendor API
